task: I need a short bio for a community class handout, and I want it to fit a plain, practical tone. Can you help me write something that gives the basics clearly and would sound natural on a program or flyer?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — I can help with that.

If you want it to sound natural on a program or flyer, keep it to:
- your name
- what you do
- your background or experience
- why you’re teaching the class
- one plain sentence about your approach

### Simple bio template
**[Name]** has experience in [field/skill] and has spent [time/years] working with [topic, trade, or group]. He/She/They teaches practical, hands-on skills with a focus on clear steps, safety, and real-world use. [Name] enjoys helping others build confidence and learn useful skills they can apply right away.

### Slightly more personal version
**[Name]** has worked in [field/skill] for [time/years] and enjoys teaching practical, hands-on lessons that are easy to follow. The focus is on clear instruction, safety, and building confidence through real-world practice. [Name] likes helping people learn skills they can actually use.

If you want, send me:
- your name
- what you teach
- your background
- the tone you want

and I’ll write a finished bio for you.

user_simulator.text:
Pretty close, but that’s still a template. I need an actual bio I can use, not placeholders.

Make it short, plain, and natural for a flyer. Something like 3-4 sentences. No fluff, no marketing talk. I teach welding and machine shop in Bakersfield, and I want it to sound straightforward and professional.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a plain 3–4 sentence version you can use:

**I teach welding and machine shop in Bakersfield, with a focus on practical skills, safety, and clear instruction. I like keeping lessons straightforward so students can build confidence and learn by doing. My goal is to help people understand the work, use the tools safely, and leave with skills they can actually apply.**

If you want, I can make it:
- a little **warmer**
- a little more **formal**
- or trim it down to **2 sentences** for a flyer.

user_simulator.text: That’s usable, but it still sounds a little generic. Make it more specific to me — plain, steady, no “build confidence” stuff. I’d want it to sound like a real vocational teacher, not a brochure. Keep it to 3 sentences if you can.

turns.agent_response:
Here’s a tighter 3-sentence version:

**I teach welding and machine shop in Bakersfield. My classes are hands-on, focused on safe tool use, correct procedure, and doing the work the right way. I keep the instruction plain and practical so students know what to do and can follow it without guessing.**

If you want, I can make it sound a little more:
- **formal**
- **friendly**
- or **shop-floor plain**

user_simulator.text: Closer. Still a little too smooth though — “doing the work the right way” and “without guessing” sounds like brochure language. I’d want it a bit more matter-of-fact, maybe mention experience or the shop setting more directly, and keep it sounding like somebody who actually runs a classroom and shop every day.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a more matter-of-fact version:

**I teach welding and machine shop in Bakersfield. My classes are hands-on and built around safe tool use, basic skills, and clear shop procedures. I keep things plain and practical so students can follow the work, understand the setup, and do the job step by step.**

If you want, I can make it:
- a little more **formal**
- a little more **personal**
- or even **shorter for a flyer**

user_simulator.text: Better, but still a little polished. The last sentence especially — "follow the work, understand the setup" — sounds like it was written for a brochure. I’d want it more plain and shop-floor direct, maybe mention years in the trade or that I teach in an actual classroom/shop setting. Give me one more version, 3 sentences, less smooth, more matter-of-fact.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a plainer version:

**I teach welding and machine shop in Bakersfield. I’ve spent years working in and around shop settings and now teach students in a classroom and lab environment. My classes stay focused on safety, basic procedure, and the kind of hands-on work students need to do in a real shop.**

If you want, I can make it:
- a little **warmer**
- a little more **formal**
- or even more **shop plain**

user_simulator.text: Closer, yeah. But “in and around shop settings” is still a little vague, and “classroom and lab environment” sounds academic. I’d want it more direct — something like actual years in the trade, teaching welding and machine shop in a real shop setting, and keeping it plain and practical. One more version, same length, less polished.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a plainer 3-sentence version:

**I teach welding and machine shop in Bakersfield. I’ve spent years in the trade and now teach in a real shop setting with a focus on safety, basic procedure, and hands-on work. I keep the instruction plain and practical so students know what to do and how to do it.**

If you want, I can make it a little more **formal** or even a little more **shop-floor plain**.

user_simulator.text: Closer. The last sentence still sounds a little cleaned up — “know what to do and how to do it” is the part that reads like a flyer. I’d want it even plainer, maybe something like: I teach welding and machine shop in Bakersfield. I’ve spent years in the trade and teach in a real shop setting. My classes stay on safety, basic procedure, and hands-on work. But make it sound natural, not copied from a form.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a plainer, more natural version:

**I teach welding and machine shop in Bakersfield. I’ve spent years in the trade and now teach in a real shop setting. My classes stay on safety, basic procedure, and hands-on work.**

If you want, I can also make it:
- a little **warmer**
- a little more **formal**
- or trim it down to **2 sentences** for a flyer.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Plain enough, and it sounds like me. I can use that.
